target_source_type: Official labor/income release or micro/macro table
needed_evidence: Current 2026 income or remuneration by formal/informal status, occupational group, income decile/quintile, or household distribution.
period_requirement: Must be 2026 or explicitly current enough to test the same pressure window.
unit_requirement: Income/remuneration value, growth rate, or distribution measure; employment counts alone are insufficient.
coverage_requirement: Must separate formal/informal workers or describe household income distribution clearly enough for offset testing.
claim_it_could_upgrade: Current informal-worker income offset or current household-wide income offset.
validation_gate: Pass period, unit, and coverage alignment before any offset calculation.
rejection_rule: Reject if it only gives jobs, employment, participation, unemployment, or broad labor context without income/remuneration.

evidence_front: Current 2026 retail scanner / supermarket basket data
target_source_type: Retail scanner, supermarket chain report, market research dataset, or audited basket study
needed_evidence: SKU, brand, quantity, unit size, ticket, basket composition, private-label share, cheaper-brand substitution, or purchase frequency data.
period_requirement: Must cover 2026 or the exact current pressure window.
unit_requirement: Behavioral purchase unit: SKU, basket, quantity, ticket, brand tier, unit size, or frequency.
coverage_requirement: Must identify household/consumer purchasing behavior, not only retailer nominal sales or price lists.
validation_gate: Must show observed substitution, quantity reduction, smaller-unit movement, brand switching, or basket recomposition.
rejection_rule: Reject price-only data, nominal sales-only data, or unmethoded anecdotes as trade-down proof.

target_source_type: Household expenditure survey, consumption survey, official statistical release, or research dataset
needed_evidence: Quantities, categories, basket composition, substitutions, smaller units, skipped categories, reduced frequency, or real volume changes.
period_requirement: Must cover 2026 or the current pressure window.
unit_requirement: Real consumption, quantities, volumes, category shares, or household basket behavior.
claim_it_could_upgrade: Household behavior change or direct trade-down.
validation_gate: Must distinguish price effect from quantity or composition effect.
rejection_rule: Reject if it only reports inflation, nominal spending, or macro consumption without household behavior detail.

evidence_front: Current consumer survey with explicit substitution questions
target_source_type: Consumer confidence survey, university survey, NGO survey, polling firm survey, market research survey
needed_evidence: Direct questions about cheaper brands, smaller quantities, substitutes, skipped categories, reduced consumption, or reduced purchase frequency.
period_requirement: Must be current 2026 or clearly tied to the current pressure window.
unit_requirement: Survey responses with sample size, field dates, question wording, and method details.
coverage_requirement: Must identify the surveyed population and sampling method.
claim_it_could_upgrade: Direct household trade-down context or direct behavior evidence depending on method quality.
validation_gate: Question wording must directly ask about substitution or consumption adjustment.
rejection_rule: Reject vague sentiment-only surveys as trade-down proof.

evidence_front: Official or audited food basket affordability update
target_source_type: Official food basket, basic basket, poverty line, or affordability report
needed_evidence: Current basket cost and income/earnings relationship by household type or income group.
period_requirement: Must cover 2026 or current pressure window.
unit_requirement: Basket cost plus income/earnings denominator; price-only basket data is insufficient for offset.
coverage_requirement: Must indicate household group, income group, or worker group if used for distributional claims.
claim_it_could_upgrade: Food affordability context; possibly income-pressure test if paired with current income denominator.
validation_gate: Must include both numerator and denominator for affordability/offset claims.
rejection_rule: Reject price-only basket updates as proof of household offset or failure.

evidence_front: MIPYME delinquency, financing, and repayment stress update
target_source_type: Banking, regulator, association, or credit-market report
needed_evidence: Current financing costs, repayment stress, delinquency, refinancing, FX exposure, or credit tightening for small businesses.
period_requirement: Must be current 2026 or comparable with the pressure window.
unit_requirement: Rates, delinquency, repayment, credit volume, FX exposure, or financing conditions.
coverage_requirement: Must specifically cover MIPYMES or relevant small-business segments.
validation_gate: Must remain a business-pressure claim unless connected to household behavior by direct evidence.
rejection_rule: Reject as trade-down proof unless linked to household purchasing behavior.

target_source_type: Budget execution, transfer program data, subsidy line, or public spending report
needed_evidence: Current targeted household-facing support, transfer amounts, beneficiary coverage, food/poverty support, and comparison to debt/other expenditure pressures.
period_requirement: Must be current 2026 or comparable across same months.
unit_requirement: Program amount, growth, beneficiary count, or execution line.
coverage_requirement: Must distinguish household-facing support from broad spending increases.
validation_gate: Must identify whether support is broad, targeted, rising, falling, or uneven.
rejection_rule: Reject total spending growth as proof of household cushioning.

evidence_front: Remittance and external inflow distribution bridge
target_source_type: Official remittance data, household survey, financial inclusion report, or distributional study
needed_evidence: Who receives remittances/external inflows, by income group, region, household type, or consumption use.
period_requirement: Must be current or close enough to contextualize the pressure window.
unit_requirement: Recipient distribution, amount distribution, household use, or income-group incidence.
coverage_requirement: Must connect macro inflows to household distribution, not just total inflow.
validation_gate: Must bridge macro inflow to household incidence.
rejection_rule: Reject aggregate inflows as proof of household-wide comfort.
